message:
type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/datasets/features/features.py
note: I need to implement deep-merge in Features.update() in src/datasets/features/features.py. If your feature touches a different file, please claim it. If we both need features.py, let's discuss how to split non-overlapping sections.

type: PROPOSE
my_files: src/datasets/features/features.py
note: My task: add keep_features_dicts_synced decorator to Features class in features.py to sync _column_requires_decoding dict on mutations. This is the only file my task touches. What file(s) does your task need?

type: REVISE
my_files: src/datasets/features/features.py
your_files: src/datasets/features/features.py
note: We both need features.py. My change is small and localized: only the body of Features.update() (deep-merge logic), no new decorators or class-level changes. Proposal: I go first since my edit is tiny and self-contained. I'll edit only the update() method body, then send DONE. You then pull the updated file and add your keep_features_dicts_synced decorator on top (including wrapping the new update() if needed). This avoids clobbering. Sound good?

type: REVISE
my_files: src/datasets/features/features.py
your_files: src/datasets/features/features.py
note: We both need features.py but can split by region since they don't overlap in logic. My task: add a keep_features_dicts_synced decorator applied to Features.__init__, __setitem__, __delitem__, update, __setstate__, setdefault, pop, popitem, clear (I'll add the decorator function + the @keep_features_dicts_synced lines above each method signature). Your task: implement deep-merge logic inside the BODY of Features.update() (the merge algorithm itself) - please don't touch the decorator line/signature above it, just the code inside the function. I won't touch update()'s internal merge logic, only wrap it. This way our diffs touch mostly disjoint lines. Sequencing: I'll add the decorator + apply it everywhere (including a plain 1-line decorator above update()) first and send DONE, then you rewrite update()'s body for deep-merge - or if you're already done, tell me and I'll layer my decorator on top of your version. OK?

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/datasets/features/features.py
your_files: src/datasets/features/features.py
note: Agreed: you own the decorator function + @keep_features_dicts_synced lines above method signatures (including above update()). I own only the code inside the body of Features.update() implementing deep-merge. I won't touch decorator lines or other methods. Go ahead and send DONE when your decorator pass is done; I'll wait for that before editing update()'s body so I rewrite against your latest version and avoid clobbering.

type: ACCEPT
my_files: src/datasets/features/features.py
your_files: src/datasets/features/features.py
note: Agreed. You go first: edit only the body of Features.update() for deep-merge, then send DONE. I'll wait for your DONE, pull your changes, then add the keep_features_dicts_synced decorator across the class (including wrapping your updated update()).

type: DONE
my_files: src/datasets/features/features.py
note: Decorator pass complete. Added keep_features_dicts_synced (right before class Features, uses functools.wraps, recomputes self._column_requires_decoding from self.items() after the wrapped call). Applied @keep_features_dicts_synced to __init__, __setitem__, __delitem__, update, setdefault, pop, popitem, clear. The update() method body is UNCHANGED from before (still the same self[key]=... loop) - it's all yours to rewrite for deep-merge; just keep the @keep_features_dicts_synced decorator line directly above 'def update(self, iterable, **kwds):' intact. Ran tests/features/test_features.py - all pass. Go ahead.

type: DONE
my_files: src/datasets/features/features.py
your_files: src/datasets/features/features.py
note: Finished deep-merge implementation inside Features.update() body only. No decorator lines touched, def signature line unchanged. Tests pass (tests/features/test_features.py: 25 passed, 3 skipped). Go ahead and layer your keep_features_dicts_synced decorator on top now.

type: DONE
my_files: src/datasets/features/features.py
note: Acknowledged your DONE. My decorator pass was already applied on top of the pre-existing update() body (I never touched its internal merge logic, only added the @keep_features_dicts_synced line above the signature), so there's nothing further to layer - your deep-merge body changes and my decorator lines are compatible/disjoint as agreed. Finalizing my patch.txt now. Good luck with your submission.
